businessRules.rule: A slot cannot be booked by more than one customer simultaneously.
businessRules.rationale: Prevents double-booking regardless of concurrent requests.
businessRules.enforcement: Unique database constraint on (tenant_id, staff_id, slot_date, slot_start) in slot_locks table.

businessRules.rule: A customer may only cancel an appointment up to the number of hours defined in the tenant's cancellation policy before the appointment start time.
businessRules.rationale: Protects tenant revenue from last-minute cancellations.
businessRules.enforcement: Validated in BookingCommandService before writing appointment.cancelled event.

businessRules.rule: Staff cannot be booked outside their configured working hours.
businessRules.rationale: Ensures staff availability respects business operating constraints.
businessRules.enforcement: Availability Service computes open slots only within tenant_working_hours.
exceptions: Admin can override with a manual slot creation in future versions

businessRules.rule: A booking saga must be atomic — either all steps succeed (slot reserved + payment charged + event confirmed) or all compensations are applied (slot released and/or charge reversed).
businessRules.rationale: Prevents partial bookings where a slot is held but no appointment exists, or payment is charged without confirmation.
businessRules.enforcement: Booking Orchestrator compensates synchronously on any step failure.

businessRules.rule: A tenant's data must never be readable by another tenant under any condition, including infrastructure failure or application bug.
businessRules.enforcement: PostgreSQL Row-Level Security enforced at DB layer; tenant_id on every table; RLS safe-by-default (null context = zero rows).

businessRules.rule: Duplicate booking requests carrying the same idempotency key must produce exactly one confirmed appointment.
businessRules.rationale: Network retries must not result in double-charges or duplicate appointments.
businessRules.enforcement: booking_idempotency table with unique constraint on idempotency_key.

businessRules.rule: A notification delivery failure must never block, delay, or roll back the booking operation that triggered it.
businessRules.rationale: Core booking availability must not depend on third-party email/SMS providers.
businessRules.enforcement: Notification Service is a fully decoupled async subscriber. Retries are isolated to the notification pipeline.

businessRules.rule: A customer may not have more active bookings than the tenant's configured max_advance_bookings limit.
businessRules.rationale: Prevents a single customer from monopolising availability.
businessRules.enforcement: Validated in BookingCommandService before initiating saga.

businessRules.rule: When a cancellation triggers a refund, the refund failure must not prevent slot release or customer notification.
businessRules.rationale: Slot must be freed immediately. Refund failure is handled separately via DLQ and manual ops.
businessRules.enforcement: Cancellation Saga choreography — each step is independent with its own failure path.

businessRules.rule: Appointments may only be booked within the tenant's configured booking window.
businessRules.rationale: Prevents bookings too far in advance that may overwhelm the calendar.
businessRules.enforcement: Availability Service excludes slots beyond tenant_config.booking_window_days.

businessRules.rule: A suspended tenant's data must remain read-accessible to the tenant admin for 30 days before deprovisioning.
businessRules.rationale: Allows data export before permanent loss of access.
businessRules.enforcement: Status machine — suspended state permits read-only access. Deprovisioning requires explicit operator action after 30 days.

businessRules.rule: All tenant data must be retained for a minimum of 90 days after deprovisioning before hard deletion.
Every event must carry a tenant_id, event_id, correlation_id, and occurred_at in its envelope.
businessRules.rationale: Enables tenant isolation, deduplication, and distributed tracing across all consumers.
businessRules.enforcement: EventEnvelope validator in all consumers — routes to DLQ if envelope is invalid.

businessRules.rule: All write operations that modify tenant-scoped data must produce an immutable audit log entry.
businessRules.rationale: GDPR and compliance requirement. Enables incident forensics.
businessRules.enforcement: audit_log table with INSERT-only privileges for booking_app_user.
